description_long: This is a tall, ornate clock tower reminiscent of the iconic Elizabeth Tower (commonly known as Big Ben) in London. The structure features a square base with vertical stonework and decorative horizontal bands, rising to a prominent clock face on each side. The clock faces are circular with Roman numerals, surrounded by intricate gold detailing and set within a square frame. Above the clocks, the tower transitions into a steep, pyramidal spire with a metallic or stone finish, topped by a slender finial. The overall color palette includes dark stone, white clock faces, and gold accents, giving the tower a stately and historic appearance. The design suggests a robust stone or masonry construction, typical of public landmarks in urban environments.
description: A tall, ornate stone clock tower with four large Roman numeral clock faces and a steep pyramidal spire, featuring gold and white decorative elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing a detailed clock face with Roman numerals, gold trim, and the full height of the tower's stonework and spire.
description_view_1: Side view nearly identical to the front, with the same clock face and architectural details visible.
description_view_2: Another side view, mirroring the previous views with the same clock face and spire details.
description_view_3: Rear view, visually similar to the other sides, again showing the clock face and ornate spire.
attribute_clock_face_design: The clock faces are large, circular, and set within a square frame, featuring black Roman numerals on a white background. Each face has two black hands (hour and minute), gold decorative trim around the edge, and a patterned border. The design is ornate and traditional, with a clear, legible layout.
